title: SK Hynix’s HBM Empire Powers 65% US Revenue — Is This the Must-Own AI Stock?
excerpt: While investors chase GPU makers, a South Korean chipmaker has quietly locked up the single most critical chokepoint in AI infrastructure, and its latest rev...
content_html:
Rich Duprey

Tue, July 21, 2026 at 10:31 AM CDT 4 min read

Quick Read

  • SK Hynix commands 58% of global HBM revenue, with sales more than doubling year-over-year to fuel a record $33 billion operating profit in FY2025.

  • The U.S. now generates 65% of SK Hynix's revenue, anchored by a Nvidia partnership that alone contributed 24% of total 2025 sales.

  • HBM's structural supply shortages provide more pricing durability than typical memory downturns, but heavy Nvidia dependence remains a real concentration risk.

  • Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and SK Hynix didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

The artificial intelligence revolution has transformed the semiconductor landscape in ways few predicted even two years ago. What started as hype around chatbots has become a voracious appetite for data center infrastructure, where memory chips -- especially high-bandwidth memory, or HBM -- serve as the critical bridge between processors and massive datasets. 

Global memory demand has exploded, with prices for key products rising sharply and suppliers racing to keep up. In this environment, SK Hynix (NASDAQ:SKHY) has emerged as a standout player, leveraging its HBM expertise to capture substantial market share while shifting its revenue base heavily toward the U.S. For everyday investors seeking exposure to AI without chasing every hot GPU name, this South Korean chipmaker offers a compelling, data-backed story worth examining closely.

HBM Leadership Drives Premium Performance

SK Hynix doesn't lead the entire DRAM market, but it dominates the segment that matters most for AI. In Q1 2026, the company held approximately 58% of the global HBM market by revenue, according to Counterpoint Research, well ahead of Samsung and Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) at around 21% each. HBM -- a specialized form of DRAM that stacks memory chips for faster data transfer -- powers Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) accelerators and similar AI hardware.

Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and SK Hynix didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

This leadership translates into real financial results. SK Hynix reported FY2025 revenue of $65 billion, with DRAM (heavily weighted toward HBM) contributing the lion's share at roughly $44 billion, compared to NAND flash at $21 billion. HBM sales more than doubled year-over-year, fueling record operating profit of $33 billion. In the broader DRAM market, SK Hynix captured about 29% revenue share in Q1 2026, trailing Samsung's 38% but outperforming on the high-margin AI side.

content_html_zh:
里奇·杜普雷

2026 年 7 月 21 日星期二上午 10:31 CDT 阅读时间 4 分钟

快速阅读

  • SK 海力士占据全球 HBM 收入的 58%，销售额同比增长一倍以上，推动 2025 财年营业利润达到创纪录的 330 亿美元。
  • 目前，SK 海力士 65% 的收入来自美国，其中与 Nvidia 的合作伙伴关系占 2025 年总销售额的 24%。
  • HBM 的结构性供应短缺比典型的内存衰退提供了更高的定价稳定性，但对 Nvidia 的严重依赖仍然是真正的集中风险。
  • 立即行动：2010 年致电 NVIDIA 的分析师刚刚列出了他心目中的十大人工智能股票，而 SK 海力士并未上榜。 今天免费获取名字。

人工智能革命以两年前很少有人预测的方式改变了半导体格局。 最初围绕聊天机器人的炒作已成为人们对数据中心基础设施的巨大需求，其中内存芯片（尤其是高带宽内存（HBM））充当处理器和海量数据集之间的关键桥梁。

全球内存需求激增，主要产品价格大幅上涨，供应商竞相跟上。 在这种环境下，SK 海力士（纳斯达克股票代码：SKHY）成为了一个杰出的参与者，利用其 HBM 专业知识夺取了巨大的市场份额，同时将其收入基础大量转向美国。对于寻求投资人工智能而不是追逐每个热门 GPU 名称的日常投资者来说，这家韩国芯片制造商提供了一个引人注目的、有数据支持的故事，值得仔细研究。

HBM 领先地位推动卓越绩效

SK 海力士并没有领导整个 DRAM 市场，但它在对人工智能最重要的领域占据主导地位。 根据 Counterpoint Research 的数据，2026 年第一季度，按收入计算，该公司占据全球 HBM 市场约 58% 的份额，远远领先于三星和美光科技（纳斯达克股票代码：MU），各占 21% 左右。 HBM 是 DRAM 的一种特殊形式，可堆叠内存芯片以实现更快的数据传输，为 Nvidia（纳斯达克股票代码：NVDA）加速器和类似的人工智能硬件提供动力。

立即行动：2010 年致电 NVIDIA 的分析师刚刚列出了他心目中的十大人工智能股票，而 SK 海力士并未上榜。 今天免费获取名字。

这种领导力转化为实际的财务业绩。 SK 海力士报告 2025 财年收入为 650 亿美元，其中 DRAM（主要是 HBM）贡献了最大份额，约为 440 亿美元，而 NAND 闪存则为 210 亿美元。 HBM 销售额同比增长一倍以上，营业利润达到创纪录的 330 亿美元。 在更广泛的 DRAM 市场中，SK 海力士在 2026 年第一季度占据了约 29% 的收入份额，落后于三星的 38%，但在高利润的人工智能方面表现出色。
